---
title: Getting started
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Getting started}
  %\VignetteEngine{quarto::html}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
knitr:
  opts_chunk:
    collapse: true
    comment: '#>'
---

`{azkit}` is an R package to help you:

* handle authentication with Azure
* perform basic tasks accessing blob and table storage (containers)
* read in data from some common file types.

The purpose of this page is to show you a few ways you might use azkit functions
to achieve certain tasks.
It's not the only way you can set things up and use the functions, it's just an
opinionated example workflow - you may choose to do things differently 😀.

## Example workflow

Imagine you have a project where you need to read in a file that is hosted on
Azure storage.

Here are some steps you might follow.

## General setup (optional)

Open a `.Renviron` text file in your project folder.
In this file, add a line:

```
AZ_STORAGE_EP=
```
and after the `=` sign add the URL of your Azure endpoint.

## R code

The steps below are just one approach - you are free to do things your own way!

Open your preferred IDE and start an R script file or a quarto document.

```{r}
#| label: setup
library(azkit)
```
